page: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-150s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC processor. It is configured with 4 shared vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading operating at 2.0 GHz. The system includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 2934.18, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems. However, multi-core performance is more moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 11664.13 and a PassMark CPU score of 9459.22, both falling in the middle 50%. The server exhibits average memory bandwidth, reaching 73.06 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 60.22 GB/sec for large block write operations. Tradeoffs are evident in multi-threaded decompression and database operations, where bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and PassMark database operations perform in the bottom 25%.

Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-focused workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The inclusion of 150 GB of NVMe SSD storage provides fast local I/O, though static web serving throughput is relatively low at 4.46 GB/sec. This configuration is suitable for lightweight multi-threaded applications, basic Redis caching, and small-scale text processing or low-demand utility services.
description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-150s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server utilizing an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor with 4 shared vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. It features 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and includes one public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the top 25%, while multi-core metrics and memory bandwidth remain average. Tradeoffs include weaker performance in database operations and multi-threaded decompression, which score in the bottom 25%. This shared-CPU instance provides a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring moderate compute resources and fast local storage, such as Redis caching, lightweight web serving, and basic utility applications.

tagline: An AMD EPYC virtual server featuring shared compute resources, fast NVMe storage, and strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.

8.0 GB system memory paired with 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Average memory bandwidth and Redis SET performance of 7.85 million operations per second
Suitable for lightweight caching, utility services, and basic compute tasks
